title: pfaffia pulverulenta
intro: Pfaffia pulverulenta, also known as suma or para-para in Portuguese, is a plant species native to the savannas of South America. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant is said to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as supporting the immune system and reducing stress levels. Pfaffia pulverulenta is commonly prepared as a tea or capsule, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including arthritis and gastrointestinal disorders.
